summary: Completed testing of 3 new features: 1) Infinite scroll for lists > 100 entries, 2) Status bar count fix showing N papers (M total) when period-filtered, 3) Paper categories on detail page. All 6 backend tests passed (100%). All 7 frontend UI tests passed (100%).
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/papers/:id returns categories array in paper object
Backend: Papers with multiple categories return all categories (e.g., ['physics.comp-ph', 'cond-mat.mtrl-sci', 'physics.chem-ph'])
Backend: Period filter 'recent' returns total_papers=50, leaderboard_len=23 (different counts)
Backend: Period filter 'all' returns matching total_papers and leaderboard_len
Backend: show_all with period=all returns 250 papers for infinite scroll
Backend: All papers have required fields (id, rank, title, score)
Frontend: Tag panel open with All Time shows 250 papers
Frontend: Initially renders only 50 rows (infinite scroll working)
Frontend: Status bar shows '23 papers (50 total)' when period filter reduces count
Frontend: Status bar shows '50 papers' without parenthetical when all papers displayed
Frontend: Paper detail page (/paper/:id) shows categories section
Frontend: Primary category has '(primary)' label and distinct styling (bg-primary/10, border-primary/20)
Frontend: Secondary categories have different styling (bg-secondary, no '(primary)' label)
Frontend: Paper with 3 categories displays all tags correctly
critical_code_review_comments: IntersectionObserver correctly implemented with sentinelRef and displayCount state
displayCount resets to 50 on fetchLeaderboard (line 137) - proper behavior for data changes
Status bar correctly uses leaderboard.length instead of totalPapers (line 331-332)
Paper categories displayed with Tag icon and proper primary/secondary styling (PaperPage lines 104-123)
Primary category index check (i === 0) correctly identifies primary category
seed_data_creation: No seed data created - used existing data (250 total papers, cs.RO has 50)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 9 verified: 1) Infinite scroll works via IntersectionObserver - initially renders 50 rows, loads +50 on scroll. 2) Status bar shows 'N papers (M total)' when period filter reduces displayed count. 3) Paper detail page shows all categories with primary highlighted and labeled. Test file test_infinite_scroll_status_categories.py covers all new features.
